what: Update M<=32 K>1024 fused config from legacy BSM=32/BSN=64/BSK=512/NW=8/NS=1 to BSM=8/BSN=128/BSK=256/NW=4/NS=2/.cg -- v189 tried this but LB returned 'could not parse score', so the optimization was never evaluated. This doubles grid from 112 to 224 blocks and enables software pipelining.
why: M<=32 K>1024 shapes (e.g. 32x7168x2048) use a legacy config from v21 era with 0.44 waves and no pipelining. v189 updated this but the LB submission failed to parse the score. Re-testing the same optimization to get actual performance data.

what: Use library-tuned config for split-K shape (16x2112x7168): BSK=512/NS=1/split-K=14/waves_per_eu=1 instead of current BSK=256/NS=2/split-K=7/waves_per_eu=2. AMD's gfx950-GEMM-A16WFP4_PRESHUFFLED-N=2112-K=7168.json specifies this exact config for M<=8.
why: v145 tried split-K=14 but with BSK=256/NS=2 (our current K block size). The AMD-tuned config uses BSK=512/NS=1 with split-K=14 -- a fundamentally different balance: 1 K-iteration per split with larger K-tile (512 vs 256) and no double-buffering overhead. 476 blocks (1.86 waves) vs 238 (0.93 waves). This is the config AMD engineers optimized specifically for N=2112,K=7168.
override: v145 used BSK=256/NS=2 with split-K=14, not the library BSK=512/NS=1 config

what: cache_modifier=None for M=64 fused path (from .cg). AMD library tuned config for M<=64 uses null cache modifier. .cg bypasses L1 but for M=64 the L1 may be beneficial for B tile reuse within a block.
why: The gfx950 preshuffle tuned JSON uses cache_modifier=null for M<=64 shapes. Our M=64 path uses .cg which bypasses L1 cache. For BSM=16/BSN=128, each block loads 16x256 bf16 A tile and 128x128 fp4 B tile per K-iter. With 4 warps, the B tile may benefit from L1 caching. v080 tried waves_per_eu=2 + no cache modifier but was a combo change.

what: Combo: .wt on M=256 fp4 stores + waves_per_eu=2 for M<=4 and M<=8 fused paths. M=256 .wt confirmed -1.5 to -4% BM across 23 tests. waves_per_eu=2 for M<=4/8 tested individually as neutral (v073/v107/v167/v178) but may compound with .wt.
why: Both changes are individually neutral-to-positive on BM. .wt for M=256 consistently improves that shape by -1.5 to -4% on BM but noise on other shapes masks it at geomean level. waves_per_eu=2 on M<=4/8 may help by allowing better wave scheduling. Combined effect targets both the M=256 shape and small-M shapes simultaneously.
override: Individual changes tested many times but this specific combination not tested

what_failed: 4 approaches tested: (1) M<=32 K>1024 config update (BSM=8/BSN=128) -- neutral, LB shapes all have K=512; (2) Library-tuned split-K=14/BSK=512/NS=1 for 16x2112x7168 -- 32% regression vs our tuned split-K=7/BSK=256/NS=2; (3) cache_modifier=None for M=64 fused -- slightly worse (+1.8%); (4) waves_per_eu=2 for M<=4/8 combo with .wt M=256 -- neutral (+0.2%). After 226+ versions exploring all viable paths (tile configs, split-K, cache modifiers, ASM kernels, env vars, quant alternatives, CK backend, gluon kernels, HIP native kernels), the 9.5% gap to #1 likely requires a fundamentally different algorithmic approach.
